question: Given the Ch.01 claim that NuScale eliminates LBLOCA, does Ch.05 provide adequate technical basis for this claim?

nuscale_ch01_0727.title: DSRS 15.2.8, Rev 0: Feedwater System Pipe Break Inside and Outside Containment (PWR)II.3 Calculated Site Boundary Doses
nuscale_ch01_0727.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for feedwater system pipe breaks inside and outside containment, specifically regarding calculated site boundary doses. It states that the analysis conforms to the applicable requirements, with no exceptions noted for this criterion.

nuscale_ch01_0865.title: Nuclear Power Plants to be Operated on Multi-Unit Sites
nuscale_ch01_0865.summary: This section outlines requirements for COL applicants referencing the NuScale Power Plant design certification to assess potential hazards from construction activities at multi-unit sites. Applicants must identify controls to mitigate risks and ensure that safety limits for operating units are not exceeded. These requirements do not apply to construction activities at single-unit NuScale facilities with operating modules.

nuscale_ch01_0725.title: DSRS 15.2.8, Rev 0: Feedwater System Pipe Break Inside and Outside Containment (PWR)II.1 Reactor Coolant System and Main Steam System Pressures
nuscale_ch01_0725.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for feedwater system pipe breaks inside and outside containment in a PWR. It confirms that the reactor coolant and main steam system pressures meet requirements, and for core damage potential, NuScale uses a heat generation rate limit to prevent fuel centerline melting during slower reactivity insertions. Calculated site boundary doses also conform to the relevant criteria.

nuscale_ch01_0864.title: Nuclear Power Plants to be Operated on Multi-Unit Sites
nuscale_ch01_0864.summary: This section outlines requirements for COL applicants referencing the NuScale Power Plant design certification to assess and manage potential hazards from construction activities at multi-unit sites. The evaluation must ensure that safety-related structures and systems of both existing and new units are protected, and that operational limits are not exceeded. These requirements do not apply to construction activities at a single NuScale Power Plant with operating modules.

nuscale_ch01_0726.title: DSRS 15.2.8, Rev 0: Feedwater System Pipe Break Inside and Outside Containment (PWR)II.2 Evaluation of Core Damage Potential
nuscale_ch01_0726.summary: This section confirms that NuScale’s safety analysis for a feedwater system pipe break inside and outside containment meets regulatory criteria. Specifically, for the evaluation of core damage potential, NuScale applies a heat generation rate limit during slower reactivity insertions to ensure fuel centerline melting limits are not exceeded. All relevant criteria are addressed and conformance is noted.

nuscale_ch01_0748.title: SRP 15.4.6, Rev 2: Inadvertent Decrease in Boron Concentration in the Reactor Coolant System (PWR)II.2 Fuel Cladding Integrity
nuscale_ch01_0748.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria regarding inadvertent decreases in boron concentration in the reactor coolant system, specifically addressing fuel cladding integrity. It states that NuScale’s design meets the relevant requirements, noting that Critical Heat Flux (CHF) is the appropriate term for the NuScale phenomenon, and that operator action is not required to mitigate such events.

nuscale_ch01_0747.title: SRP 15.4.6, Rev 2: Inadvertent Decrease in Boron Concentration in the Reactor Coolant System (PWR)II.1 Reactor Coolant and Main Steam System Pressures
nuscale_ch01_0747.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with SRP 15.4.6, Rev 2, regarding inadvertent decreases in boron concentration in the reactor coolant system for PWRs. It states that NuScale’s design meets the relevant regulatory criteria for reactor coolant and main steam system pressures, fuel cladding integrity, incident frequency, and analysis methods, while noting that certain requirements (such as operator action and non-safety systems) are not applicable due to design differences like the absence of reactor coolant pumps.

nuscale_ch01_0779.title: DSRS 15.6.5, Rev 0: Loss-of-Coolant Accidents Resulting from Spectrum of Postulated Piping Breaks within the Reactor Coolant Pressure BoundaryII.4 Programmatic Requirements
nuscale_ch01_0779.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for loss-of-coolant accidents (LOCA) as outlined in DSRS 15.6.5 and related SRP sections. It notes full or partial conformance with requirements for calculating radiological consequences and containment leakage, with some criteria superseded by SRP Section 15.0.3 and certain guidance applicable only to BWRs. The table documents how NuScale addresses each regulatory requirement.

nuscale_ch01_0780.title: SRP 15.6.5.A, Rev 1: Radiological Consequences of a Design Basis Loss-of-Coolant Accident Including Containment Leakage ContributionII.1 Calculated Doses and Containment Leakage Contribution
nuscale_ch01_0780.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for evaluating radiological consequences of a design basis loss-of-coolant accident (LOCA), including containment leakage. It notes that while NuScale’s analysis methods generally conform to guidance in SRP 15.6.5.A, some acceptance criteria and analysis models are superseded by SRP Section 15.0.3, and certain guidance applies only to BWRs. The section also confirms full conformance regarding engineered safety feature (ESF) system leakage assumptions.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It cites guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as specific ASME code cases relevant to reactor component materials, quality assurance, and inspection procedures.

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the Reactor Coolant System (RCS) components and subsystems in the NuScale Power Module, detailing the integration of the reactor, steam generators, pressurizer, safety and ECCS valves, and associated piping within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV). It also outlines the configuration and boundaries of the steam generator system, emphasizing the integral design of the steam generators within the RPV and the separation of primary and secondary sides.

nuscale_ch01_0005.title: Reactor Type
nuscale_ch01_0005.summary: The NuScale reactor is a small modular pressurized water reactor (PWR) featuring a cylindrical steel containment vessel designed to withstand high-pressure events and maintain a vacuum during normal operation. Each NuScale Power Module (NPM) integrates the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel, using natural circulation for coolant flow and eliminating the need for reactor coolant pumps. A NuScale plant can include up to 12 modules, each producing 160 MWt (about 50 MWe), for a total net output of approximately 570 MWe for a 12-module facility.

nuscale_ch01_0776.title: DSRS 15.6.5, Rev 0: Loss-of-Coolant Accidents Resulting from Spectrum of Postulated Piping Breaks within the Reactor Coolant Pressure BoundaryII.1 Evaluation of ECCS Performance
nuscale_ch01_0776.summary: This section evaluates NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for loss-of-coolant accidents (LOCAs) and related radiological consequences. It notes that while some guidance and technical specifications are applicable, certain radiological acceptance criteria are superseded by more current regulatory sections (SRP 15.0.3). Additionally, the NuScale design justifies exemptions from parts of 10 CFR 50 Appendix K, incorporating only technically relevant requirements for small break LOCA analysis.

nuscale_ch05_0011.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Inservice Inspection and Testing
nuscale_ch05_0011.summary: This section describes the materials and standards used for threaded fasteners in key reactor coolant system components, emphasizing the use of Alloy 718 for its compatibility and stress corrosion cracking resistance. It also outlines the requirements for preservice and inservice inspection and testing of pressure-retaining components, specifying adherence to the latest ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) editions as mandated by regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0060.title: NuScale Integral System Test Program
nuscale_ch01_0060.summary: The NuScale Integral System Test Program was established to collect thermal-hydraulic data for system characterization and to validate safety analysis codes using a scaled model of the NuScale Power Module (NPM) design. The NIST-1 facility replicates key reactor systems and supports the development and validation of proprietary safety codes (NRELAP5 and PIM), ensuring accurate modeling of the NPM’s unique features. Testing at NIST-1, located at Oregon State University, has been integral to NuScale’s design certification process.

nuscale_ch01_0743.title: SRP 15.4.4-15.4.5, Rev 2: Startup of an Inactive Loop or Recirculation Loop at an Incorrect Temperature, and Flow Controller Malfunction Causing an Increase in BWR Core Flow RateII.C Events of Moderate Frequency
nuscale_ch01_0743.summary: This section explains that the regulatory guidance in SRP 15.4.4-15.4.5, which addresses startup of an inactive loop or recirculation loop at incorrect temperature and flow controller malfunctions in PWRs and BWRs, is not applicable to the NuScale reactor design because it does not use reactor coolant pumps or forced coolant flow. Instead, any relevant postulated startup reactivity accidents for NuScale are addressed under DSRS 15.4.6, which covers inadvertent decreases in boron concentration.

nuscale_ch01_0744.title: SRP 15.4.4-15.4.5, Rev 2: Startup of an Inactive Loop or Recirculation Loop at an Incorrect Temperature, and Flow Controller Malfunction Causing an Increase in BWR Core Flow RateII.D Instrument Setpoints
nuscale_ch01_0744.summary: This section explains that the regulatory guidance in SRP 15.4.4-15.4.5 regarding startup of inactive or recirculation loops and flow controller malfunctions is not applicable to the NuScale reactor design, as it does not use reactor coolant pumps or forced coolant flow like traditional PWRs. Instead, NuScale addresses potential startup reactivity accidents under DSRS 15.4.6, which covers inadvertent decreases in boron concentration.

nuscale_ch01_0659.title: SRP 15.0.2, (March 2007): Review of Transient and Accident Analysis MethodsII.4 Uncertainty Analysis
nuscale_ch01_0659.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for transient and accident analysis methods, noting partial or non-applicability where NuScale’s design differs from traditional requirements (such as using CHF instead of DNBR and a modified AST methodology). It highlights that NuScale seeks exemptions from certain 10 CFR 50 Appendix K requirements, focusing its evaluation models only on technically relevant features for its unique design.

nuscale_ch01_0657.title: SRP 15.0.2, (March 2007): Review of Transient and Accident Analysis MethodsII.2 Accident Scenario Identification Process
nuscale_ch01_0657.summary: This section summarizes NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria for accident scenario identification and analysis methods. It notes that NuScale's design generally conforms to SRP 15.0.2 requirements for accident scenario identification, with some departures or exemptions from certain BWR-specific or radiological analysis provisions, as NuScale uses methodologies more applicable to its unique design. The evaluation models focus on technically relevant features required by regulations, supporting exemptions where appropriate.

edges.description: Section A discusses NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria for specific accident scenarios, while Section B outlines a COL applicant's obligation to evaluate hazards from multi-unit site construction, connecting them through the shared context of regulatory compliance for plant safety but not directly addressing the LBLOCA elimination claim.

Section A references and summarizes the technical evaluation criteria from Section B to demonstrate NuScale's conformance with regulatory requirements for feedwater system pipe breaks, thereby using B's details as the technical basis for A's claims.

Section A explains that certain SRP guidance is not applicable to NuScale due to its unique design (lack of reactor coolant pumps), while Section B demonstrates how NuScale conforms to DSRS criteria for other accident scenarios, together providing evidence that NuScale's design features (such as elimination of LBLOCA contributors) are addressed in the technical basis.

Section A explains that certain PWR-specific accident scenarios (e.g., those involving reactor coolant pumps and loops) are not applicable to NuScale due to its unique design, while Section B documents NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria for other accident scenarios, together supporting the claim that traditional LBLOCA scenarios are eliminated or not applicable.

Section A explains why certain PWR-specific accident scenarios (relying on reactor coolant pumps and loops) are not applicable to NuScale due to its unique design, while Section B documents NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria for other accident scenarios, together providing a technical basis that supports the claim in Ch.01 that NuScale eliminates LBLOCA by design.
